difference_records.right_value: The procedure begins at a single start state. A node (N1) expands that state into multiple parallel paths (N2A, N2B, N2C). All paths converge at a node (N3) that introduces a differentiating input. A sorting/ranking node (N4) then re-evaluates path fit given that input. N4 has three possible exits: if separation is weak (threshold not met), control passes to N5 which loops back to reopen the multi-path stage; if one path is clearly strongest, control passes to N6 which closes the procedure; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends immediately. The loop via N5 can repeat the expand-converge-differentiate-sort cycle until one of the two terminal conditions is met.

All paths converge at a node (N3) that introduces a differentiating input. A sorting/ranking node (N4) then re-evaluates path fit given that input. N4 has three possible exits: if separation is weak (threshold not met), control passes to N5 which loops back to reopen the multi-path stage; if one path is clearly strongest, control passes to N6 which closes the procedure; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends immediately. The loop via N5 can repeat the expand-converge-differentiate-sort cycle until one of the two terminal conditions is met.

These paths converge at N3, which introduces a differentiating input. At N4, the path fit is re-sorted based on this new input. From N4, one of three conditional branches is taken: if the threshold is not met, execution moves to N5, which reopens continuation due to weak separation and loops back to the paths N2A/N2B/N2C; if one path is selected, it goes to N6 and terminates at END; if continuation is not justified, it goes straight to END.

If one path is clearly dominant, the procedure closes through V3 and exits. If continuation is determined to be unjustified, the procedure exits directly from X1 without selection. The loop has no stated upper bound.

Node X1 evaluates the paths by re-sorting their fit given the new input. Depending on the evaluation at X1, the procedure branches three ways: if continuation is not justified, execution terminates directly; if one path is selected as the strongest, node V3 closes the process and it ends; if the threshold is not met due to weak separation, node Q8 reopens continuation and loops back to K2, M9, and R4 for further cycling.

If separation among paths remains weak and the threshold is not met, the process transitions to N5, which reopens continuation by routing back through pass-through states N5a, N5b, and N5c to the N2 nodes, establishing an evaluation loop.

If one path is clearly dominant, the procedure closes through V3 and exits. If separation among paths remains weak, a re-evaluation node (Q8) reopens one or more of the candidate paths and the loop repeats. If continuation is determined to be unjustified, the procedure exits directly from X1 without selection. The loop has no stated upper bound.

difference_records.left_value: The procedure begins at a single start state. A node (N1) expands that state into multiple parallel paths (N2A, N2B, N2C). All paths converge at a node (N3) that introduces a differentiating input. A sorting/ranking node (N4) then re-evaluates path fit given that input. N4 has three possible exits: if separation is weak (threshold not met), control passes to N5 which loops back to reopen the multi-path stage; if one path is clearly strongest, control passes to N6 which closes the procedure; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends immediately. The loop via N5 can repeat the expand-converge-differentiate-sort cycle until one of the two terminal conditions is met.
difference_records.right_value: The procedure starts by expanding a single state into three paths, each routed through an inert pass-through state into its own processing node (N2A, N2B, N2C). All three paths converge at N3, which introduces a differentiating input. N4 then re-sorts how well the paths fit given that new input. From N4 there are three outcomes: if a threshold is not met and separation among paths remains weak, N5 reopens continuation and routes back through a second set of pass-through states into the same three processing nodes, repeating the differentiate-and-re-sort cycle; if one path stands out as strongest, N6 closes the procedure and it ends; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends directly without the closing step.
difference_records.explanation: Summaries differ in compression or prose detail.

All three paths converge at a step that introduces a differentiating input (T6). A sorting and evaluation step (X1) then re-ranks the paths based on the new input. If one path is clearly dominant, the procedure closes through V3 and exits. If separation among paths remains weak, a re-evaluation node (Q8) reopens one or more of the candidate paths and the loop repeats. If continuation is determined to be unjustified, the procedure exits directly from X1 without selection. The loop has no stated upper bound.
difference_records.right_value: The procedure starts from a single state, expands it into three candidate paths (N2A, N2B, N2C), and converges them into a step that adds a differentiating input (N3). A re-sorting step (N4) then re-evaluates how well each path fits given the new input. From N4, three outcomes are possible: if one path is clearly strongest, a closing step (N6) selects it and the procedure ends; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends directly; if the threshold for separation is not met, a reopening step (N5) sends the process back through the candidate paths to gather another differentiating input and re-sort again. This loop repeats until one of the two termination conditions is reached.
difference_records.explanation: Summaries differ in compression or prose detail.

difference_records.left_value: The procedure begins by expanding a single input state into three candidate paths (K2, M9, R4). All three paths converge at a step that introduces a differentiating input (T6). A sorting and evaluation step (X1) then re-ranks the paths based on the new input. If one path is clearly dominant, the procedure closes through V3 and exits. If separation among paths remains weak, a re-evaluation node (Q8) reopens one or more of the candidate paths and the loop repeats. If continuation is determined to be unjustified, the procedure exits directly from X1 without selection. The loop has no stated upper bound.
difference_records.right_value: The procedure starts by expanding a single state into three paths, each routed through an inert pass-through state into its own processing node (N2A, N2B, N2C). All three paths converge at N3, which introduces a differentiating input. N4 then re-sorts how well the paths fit given that new input. From N4 there are three outcomes: if a threshold is not met and separation among paths remains weak, N5 reopens continuation and routes back through a second set of pass-through states into the same three processing nodes, repeating the differentiate-and-re-sort cycle; if one path stands out as strongest, N6 closes the procedure and it ends; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends directly without the closing step.
difference_records.explanation: Summaries differ in compression or prose detail.
